---
pagetitle: "Architecture 2.0 - AI-assisted computer architecture"
description: "A synthesis lecture and practical design-loop card for auditable AI-assisted work in computer architecture, hardware/software co-design, and computing-system synthesis."
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="site-wrap">
<section class="site-hero hub-hero">
  <div>
    <div class="hub-kicker">Academic hub for AI-assisted computer architecture</div>
    <h1>Architecture&nbsp;2.0</h1>
    <p class="subtitle">
      AI-assisted computer architecture.
    </p>

    <p class="site-lede">
    A public home for the design-loop card, book, tool registry, reading list,
    and workshops behind auditable AI-assisted loops for chips,
    accelerators, memory systems, simulators, compilers, benchmarks, tools, and
    evidence workflows. As AI enters these architecture loops, the pressing
    question is no longer whether a model can propose a design, but whether we
    can trust the loop that produced it.
    </p>

    <nav class="site-actions" aria-label="Primary">
      <a class="site-action" href="/start.html">Use the design-loop card <span class="arr" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span></a>
      <a class="site-action secondary" href="/book/index.html">Read the book <span class="arr" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span></a>
      <a class="site-action tertiary" href="/tools/index.html">Browse tools</a>
    </nav>
  </div>

  <div class="hub-panel" role="complementary" aria-label="Architecture 2.0 resources">
    <div class="hub-panel-header">
      <p class="hub-panel-title">Featured resources</p>
      <span class="hub-status">Work in progress</span>
    </div>
    <div class="hub-resource-list">
      <a class="hub-resource" href="/start.html">
        <span class="resource-icon" aria-hidden="true">DC</span>
        <span><strong>Design-loop card</strong><span>Draft and validate a Level 0 card in 30 minutes.</span></span>
        <span class="resource-arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
      </a>
      <a class="hub-resource" href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/tree/main/labs">
        <span class="resource-icon" aria-hidden="true">LB</span>
        <span><strong>Companion labs</strong><span>Run a separate tool-backed path and inspect the runnable receipt.</span></span>
        <span class="resource-arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
      </a>
      <a class="hub-resource" href="/tools/index.html">
        <span class="resource-icon" aria-hidden="true">TL</span>
        <span><strong>Tool registry</strong><span>Simulators, proxy models, verification harnesses, and benchmarks.</span></span>
        <span class="resource-arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
      </a>
      <a class="hub-resource" href="/readings.html">
        <span class="resource-icon" aria-hidden="true">RD</span>
        <span><strong>Reading list</strong><span>Papers, SIGARCH posts, talks, and workshop writeups.</span></span>
        <span class="resource-arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
      </a>
      <a class="hub-resource" href="/workshops.html">
        <span class="resource-icon" aria-hidden="true">WS</span>
        <span><strong>Workshop tracker</strong><span>Community venues where Architecture 2.0 work can be shared.</span></span>
        <span class="resource-arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
      </a>
      <a class="hub-resource" href="/book/index.html">
        <span class="resource-icon" aria-hidden="true">BK</span>
        <span><strong>Synthesis lecture</strong><span>Design loops, evidence, rejection, and architect-owned judgment.</span></span>
        <span class="resource-arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
      </a>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="site-section">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2>Start here</h2>
  </div>
  <div class="resource-grid">
    <a class="resource-card" href="/start.html">
      <span class="site-pill hot">Start</span>
      <h3>The design-loop card</h3>
      <p>Download the versioned card, bound one decision, and validate a Level 0 draft in 30 minutes.</p>
      <span class="resource-card-footer">Draft Level 0</span>
    </a>
    <a class="resource-card" href="/book/index.html">
      <span class="site-pill">Book</span>
      <h3>The book</h3>
      <p>The synthesis lecture defining the state, action, rejection, and evidence a trustworthy computing-system design loop requires.</p>
      <span class="resource-card-footer">Read online</span>
    </a>
    <a class="resource-card" href="/tools/index.html">
      <span class="site-pill">Tools</span>
      <h3>The tool registry</h3>
      <p>A curated registry of simulators, proxy models, verification harnesses, benchmarks, and data representations.</p>
      <span class="resource-card-footer">Browse tools</span>
    </a>
    <a class="resource-card" href="/readings.html">
      <span class="site-pill">Readings</span>
      <h3>The reading list</h3>
      <p>A curated path through papers, SIGARCH posts, talks, datasets, and workshop writeups around Architecture 2.0.</p>
      <span class="resource-card-footer">Read papers and posts</span>
    </a>
    <a class="resource-card" href="/workshops.html">
      <span class="site-pill">Workshops</span>
      <h3>The workshop tracker</h3>
      <p>A record of relevant workshops, including current participation status and prior events.</p>
      <span class="resource-card-footer">See venues</span>
    </a>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="site-section">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2>How to participate</h2>
    <p>Start with the shared review artifact, then use the registries and discussions to make related work easier to inspect.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="pathway-list">
    <div class="pathway-step">
      <span>01</span>
      <h3>Complete a card</h3>
      <p>Make one design loop and its commitment boundary inspectable.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="pathway-step">
      <span>02</span>
      <h3>Find tools and venues</h3>
      <p>The registries point newcomers to papers, working software, benchmarks, workshops, and artifact spaces.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="pathway-step">
      <span>03</span>
      <h3>Submit visible work</h3>
      <p>Structured GitHub templates turn a submission into a reviewable PR and a public project card.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="pathway-step">
      <span>04</span>
      <h3>Discuss and review</h3>
      <p>Use GitHub Discussions for questions, proposals, and review feedback that should remain searchable.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

</div>
```
